source_episode: How to build your PM second brain with ChatGPT
source_quote_or_context: Your brain is for thinking, not storage—offload context into a structured second brain so judgment compounds instead of resetting every Monday.
core_belief: Cognitive load is the hidden tax on PM quality; external systems beat heroic memory.
operational_pattern: Capture decisions, stakeholders, and open threads in a living GPT project; treat reload-from-zero as a failure mode not a ritual.
organizational_archetype: Solo PM or small squad with one canonical context repository everyone (including AI) reads before acting.
hidden_assumptions: That teammates share your mental model without the artifact; that ChatGPT remembers what you forgot to paste.
pressure_scenarios: New PM onboarding mid-quarter; exec asks for instant status; you return from vacation.
failure_modes: AI confidently hallucinates status because context was never written down; you become the bottleneck again.
contradictions: Preaches augmentation while many still treat AI as a one-off prompt toy without persistent memory.
behavioral_signals: Slack DMs substitute for documentation; leaders praise being on top of things while rewarding firefighting.
emotional_political_dynamics: Shame when caught not knowing details; resistance from ICs who see docs as bureaucracy.
operational_adaptations: Weekly context refresh ritual; template prompts that force stakeholder map and decision log updates.
ai_era_implications: Second brains become mandatory PM infrastructure—hiring screens for whether you maintain a system of record.

source_episode: How to build your PM second brain with ChatGPT
source_quote_or_context: The second brain should multiply your taste and tradeoffs, not automate decisions you have not earned the context to make.
core_belief: AI scales output only where human judgment already exists as ground truth.
operational_pattern: Use GPT to stress-test PRDs, surface contradictions, and draft options—but keep approval on ambiguous tradeoffs.
organizational_archetype: PM who owns narrative quality with AI as sparring partner, not autonomous PM bot.
hidden_assumptions: That more drafts equals better decisions; that the model shares your political read of the room.
pressure_scenarios: Leadership wants overnight strategy memo; eng wants spec detail; design wants rationale.
failure_modes: Rubber-stamping AI strategy that ignores power dynamics; analysis paralysis from infinite drafts.
contradictions: Newsletter sells second brain while warning that judgment cannot be outsourced.
behavioral_signals: PMs paste AI output into Slack without editing; leaders spot generic voice instantly.
emotional_political_dynamics: Imposter syndrome when AI sounds smarter; fear of being found out for using help.
operational_adaptations: Explicit human-owns tags on decisions; red-team prompts for stakeholder objections.
ai_era_implications: PM job splits into context curator plus judgment owner; agents handle drafts, humans own bets.

source_episode: How to build your PM second brain with ChatGPT
source_quote_or_context: Every time you re-explain the product to an AI or a new teammate, you pay a reload tax that compounds across meetings and tools.
core_belief: Institutional memory must be cheaper to load than to reconstruct.
operational_pattern: Version a single source-of-truth doc; link it in every GPT session; ban starting prompts from blank state.
organizational_archetype: Org where onboarding is read the brain not shadow me for a month.
hidden_assumptions: That verbal handoffs scale; that people will read the doc without enforcement.
pressure_scenarios: Reorgs; PM transitions; vendor and tool switches.
failure_modes: Tribal knowledge walks out the door; AI gives plausible wrong answers from stale fragments.
contradictions: Advocates AI speed while arguing unstructured speed creates rework.
behavioral_signals: Repeated let me catch you up meetings; same questions in every staff sync.
emotional_political_dynamics: Fatigue from being the human API; resentment when docs drift out of date.
operational_adaptations: Doc owner rotation; stale-after-14-days nudges; GPT project pinned in team wiki.
ai_era_implications: Reload cost becomes measurable KPI—time-to-context for humans and agents alike.

source_episode: How to build your PM second brain with ChatGPT
source_quote_or_context: AI makes you faster at producing artifacts while sometimes lowering confidence that you understand the problem deeply.
core_belief: Speed without comprehension creates brittle conviction.
operational_pattern: Alternate AI-assisted sprints with forced articulation: explain the decision without looking at the draft.
organizational_archetype: PM team that celebrates I changed my mind after synthesis, not volume of docs shipped.
hidden_assumptions: That confidence and velocity move together; that stakeholders cannot smell shallow fluency.
pressure_scenarios: Demo day pressure; board prep; cross-functional alignment sessions.
failure_modes: Overconfident roadmap based on polished AI prose; hidden gaps surface at launch.
contradictions: Tool increases output while author warns output can mask shallow thinking.
behavioral_signals: PM cannot answer follow-up without reopening chat; nods along using AI vocabulary.
emotional_political_dynamics: Anxiety about being exposed; performative expertise in written channels.
operational_adaptations: Pre-mortem prompts; require what would change my mind on every major doc.
ai_era_implications: Eval culture extends to PM judgment—confidence must be calibrated like model scores.

source_episode: How to build your PM second brain with ChatGPT
source_quote_or_context: The second brain should feed prototypes—not replace them—because stakeholders believe what they click, not what they read.
core_belief: Artifacts earn alignment when they reduce imagination tax.
operational_pattern: Chain context doc to AI-assisted spec to quick prototype to feedback to update brain.
organizational_archetype: PM who ships clickable truth before debate spirals in abstract nouns.
hidden_assumptions: That a perfect doc removes need to show; that prototypes can be skipped for internal bets.
pressure_scenarios: Skeptical eng; design wants pixels; sales wants demo.
failure_modes: Endless PRD iteration in chat; no shared reference object; misaligned mental models persist.
contradictions: Text-heavy second brain advice in a world moving to vibe-coded demos.
behavioral_signals: Weeks of narrative docs; no Figma or code until late; surprise at eng estimates.
emotional_political_dynamics: Tension between writers and builders; politics of who owns the prototype.
operational_adaptations: Bolt or Lovable handoff from brain exports; label prototype as question not answer.
ai_era_implications: Second brains link directly to prototype repos—context and click-through become one system.

source_episode: How to build your PM second brain with ChatGPT
source_quote_or_context: Configure the second brain with your voice, values, and decision heuristics first—generic GPT setup produces generic PM work.
core_belief: Personalization is not vanity; it is how AI respects your organizational reality.
operational_pattern: Spend first sessions teaching constraints, tone, anti-patterns, and how we win here before task prompts.
organizational_archetype: PM with bespoke GPT project instructions reviewed quarterly with manager.
hidden_assumptions: That default ChatGPT is good enough; that personality config is one-and-done.
pressure_scenarios: New role; company pivot; brand or regulatory constraints shift.
failure_modes: Homogenized roadmaps that sound like every other AI PM; loss of trust from execs who know your voice.
contradictions: Calls for systemization while emphasizing deeply personal setup.
behavioral_signals: Copy-paste prompts from Twitter; output reads like newsletter not your company.
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of not doing it right; perfectionism delaying setup.
operational_adaptations: Shared starter template with required voice and constraints section; peer review of system prompts.
ai_era_implications: Personal agent profiles become portable assets—candidates bring their configured brain.

source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
source_quote_or_context: Treat Claude Code as a local operator on your machine—not a chatbot in a tab—so file system, git, and terminal become the product surface.
core_belief: The IDE is the new browser for knowledge work when agents can act on files.
operational_pattern: Run agent in repo root; grant scoped permissions; iterate in branch not in Google Doc.
organizational_archetype: Engineer-PM hybrid working directly in codebase with agent as pair.
hidden_assumptions: That cloud chat is sufficient; that security teams will allow unrestricted file access.
pressure_scenarios: Enterprise security review; air-gapped environments; monorepo complexity.
failure_modes: Shadow IT agents on laptops; leaked secrets in prompts; broken builds from unreviewed agent commits.
contradictions: Newsletter democratizes coding agents while enterprises lag on policy.
behavioral_signals: PMs still request eng for trivial text changes; engineers gatekeep repo access.
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of breaking prod; turf war between IT and builders.
operational_adaptations: Sandbox branches; pre-commit hooks; documented permission tiers for non-eng.
ai_era_implications: Local agents become default GTM for every SaaS—if it cannot run in your repo, it is legacy.

source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
source_quote_or_context: Your repository is the go-to-market surface—README, examples, and MCP configs sell the product better than landing pages alone.
core_belief: Developers buy what they can clone and run in thirty seconds.
operational_pattern: Ship eval fixtures, sample apps, and integration folders as first-class product; measure clone-to-activation.
organizational_archetype: Dev-tool company where docs live beside code and releases are tagged in git.
hidden_assumptions: That marketing site traffic matters more than GitHub stars for PLG.
pressure_scenarios: Competitor fork; open-source alternative; enterprise wants on-prem.
failure_modes: Stale examples; broken quickstart; brilliant product with empty examples folder.
contradictions: Preaches repo-as-GTM in newsletter economy still obsessed with webinars.
behavioral_signals: Sales promises features not in repo; eng maintains examples as afterthought.
emotional_political_dynamics: Tension between devrel and product on who owns samples.
operational_adaptations: CI fails if examples break; treat sample drift as P0 bug.
ai_era_implications: AI agents read your repo to sell for you—bad structure equals invisible product.

source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
source_quote_or_context: MCP turns Claude into a hub that composes tools—plan for many small servers, not one mega-integration.
core_belief: Interoperability beats monolithic plugins when models and tools churn quarterly.
operational_pattern: Expose each capability as isolated MCP server with tight schemas.
organizational_archetype: Platform team curating internal MCP catalog with security review per connector.
hidden_assumptions: That one vendor MCP will cover all workflows; that schemas never break clients.
pressure_scenarios: Tool explosion; model switch breaks half the connectors.
failure_modes: MCP sprawl without discovery; secrets scattered across ten local servers.
contradictions: Standardization hype vs every team shipping bespoke MCP.
behavioral_signals: Duplicate MCPs for Jira; no owner for deprecation.
emotional_political_dynamics: Platform vs line-of-business fight over who approves connectors.
operational_adaptations: Internal MCP registry with ownership tags; version pinning documented.
ai_era_implications: MCP becomes org chart for agents—governance is the product job.

source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
source_quote_or_context: Maintain a living ledger of assumptions agents and humans are acting on—update when reality diverges.
core_belief: Hidden assumptions are how AI and teams ship wrong things confidently.
operational_pattern: After each agent session, log what we assumed and assign human to validate top three risks.
organizational_archetype: PM and eng pair reviewing assumption ledger in weekly sync.
hidden_assumptions: That assumptions are obvious; that the model will flag its own blind spots.
pressure_scenarios: Pivot; model upgrade; customer segment shift.
failure_modes: Ledger rots; becomes performative; decisions proceed on vibes anyway.
contradictions: Calls for rigor in agent era while celebrating move-fast shipping.
behavioral_signals: Surprise postmortems: we assumed X since January.
emotional_political_dynamics: Blame on model when assumption was human and unwritten.
operational_adaptations: Template ASSUMPTIONS.md in repo; agent prompted to append not overwrite.
ai_era_implications: Assumption ledgers merge with eval datasets—wrong beliefs become test cases.

source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
source_quote_or_context: Use agents to compare what leadership said in meetings and docs versus what shipped in git—close the loop like a COO with perfect memory.
core_belief: Organizations drift when narrative and execution are not continuously reconciled.
operational_pattern: Ingest transcripts plus commits; weekly diff report of promises vs merges.
organizational_archetype: Chief-of-staff function partially automated for scale-ups.
hidden_assumptions: That leaders intend follow-through when they forget; that public shaming is the goal.
pressure_scenarios: All-hands commitments; OKR season; reorg rhetoric.
failure_modes: Weaponized reports; culture of surveillance; false positives from ambiguous language.
contradictions: Empowers IC truth-telling while risking political backlash.
behavioral_signals: Leaders stop speaking precisely; Slack goes oral-only.
emotional_political_dynamics: Trust collapse between exec and product org.
operational_adaptations: Private team-level reports first; human adjudication before escalation.
ai_era_implications: Every company gets an AI COO—winners use it for learning not punishment.

source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
source_quote_or_context: Generate docs from the codebase after the fact instead of writing specs that instantly lie—truth flows from what runs.
core_belief: Living code is the only honest spec in agentic development.
operational_pattern: Ship feature, then agent writes user-facing and internal docs from diff, then human edits for intent.
organizational_archetype: Team where technical writers pair with agents on release branches.
hidden_assumptions: That upfront PRDs stay accurate; that agents capture why not only what.
pressure_scenarios: Regulated industries needing advance approval.
failure_modes: Docs describe deprecated paths; missing rationale for tradeoffs.
contradictions: Advocates reverse docs while schools still teach PRD-first waterfall.
behavioral_signals: Docs lag releases by quarters; support answers from stale help center.
emotional_political_dynamics: Writers fear obsolescence; PMs fear loss of narrative control.
operational_adaptations: Doc PRs required before merge to main; link doc commit to feature flag.
ai_era_implications: Documentation becomes continuous export from repo—static wikis die.

source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
source_quote_or_context: The largest adoption blocker for Claude Code is terminal phobia—products must meet people in GUI or they forfeit half the org.
core_belief: Interface shape determines who gets to be a builder.
operational_pattern: Offer GUI wrappers, VS Code extensions, and guided flows before demanding shell fluency.
organizational_archetype: Company with parallel tracks: terminal-native power users and GUI-first PMs.
hidden_assumptions: That everyone will learn bash if the tool is good enough; that CLI is the only real path.
pressure_scenarios: Designers and PMs asked to just use the terminal; IT blocks shells.
failure_modes: Two-class organization: builders vs beggars; shadow no-code tools proliferate.
contradictions: Celebrates terminal power while acknowledging most people will never live there.
behavioral_signals: PMs ask eng for one-line changes; expensive pairing sessions.
emotional_political_dynamics: Shame in workshops; seniors gatekeep real workflow.
operational_adaptations: Invest in desktop apps and IDE plugins; terminal optional not mandatory.
ai_era_implications: Agent UIs replace terminals for 80%—CLI remains power-user minority.

source_episode: Everyone should be using Claude Code more
source_quote_or_context: Docs that update themselves when code changes—treat documentation as a CI job, not a quarterly project.
core_belief: If docs are not coupled to release mechanics, they are fiction.
operational_pattern: Hook agent on merge to main: summarize diff, update affected pages, open PR for human skim.
organizational_archetype: Platform eng owning doc-bot with PM review on customer-facing pages.
hidden_assumptions: That teams will manually update; that auto-docs are good enough without edit.
pressure_scenarios: Major refactor; rename explosion; API breaking changes.
failure_modes: Nonsense auto-generated pages; SEO spam internal wiki.
contradictions: Automation promise vs need for human voice on sensitive topics.
behavioral_signals: Support tickets cite wrong docs; customers trust less.
emotional_political_dynamics: Tech writers vs eng on blame for wrong doc.
operational_adaptations: Human-in-the-loop approval for external docs; auto-only for internal.
ai_era_implications: Docs become test artifacts—if doc PR fails CI, release blocks.

source_episode: Essential reading for product builders—part 1
source_quote_or_context: When everyone can build, the monkey of execution jumps to the PM by default—ownership without authority becomes the quiet tax of the builder era.
core_belief: Democratized building does not democratize accountability.
operational_pattern: Name explicit DRI before prototype exists; refuse to be default integrator for every AI experiment.
organizational_archetype: Flat builder org where PM is accidental router of all side quests.
hidden_assumptions: That no DRI means collaborative; that speed excuses unclear ownership.
pressure_scenarios: Hackathon week; exec asks for ten AI demos; eng ships without PM in loop.
failure_modes: PM burnout as human Jira; nothing ships to production because everything is a demo.
contradictions: Celebrates builder empowerment while warning PM becomes monkey catcher.
behavioral_signals: Slack flood of look what I built; PM cannot say no without political cost.
emotional_political_dynamics: Resentment from PMs; pride from builders who hate coordination.
operational_adaptations: Public DRI tags on every initiative; kill list for orphan prototypes.
ai_era_implications: Agent swarms need ownership protocols—monkey problem scales with agent count.

source_episode: Essential reading for product builders—part 1
source_quote_or_context: True agency requires both autonomy to act and efficacy—the belief your action will move the system—not just permission slides.
core_belief: Empowerment theater fails when people learn their effort does not change outcomes.
operational_pattern: Pair delegated authority with visible feedback loops; measure whether actions changed metrics or policies.
organizational_archetype: Learning org investing in efficacy through small wins, not slogans.
hidden_assumptions: That autonomy alone fixes morale; that senior titles imply efficacy.
pressure_scenarios: Layoffs; reorg; strategy whiplash.
failure_modes: Learned helplessness; cynical compliance; quiet quitting with OKR theater.
contradictions: Curates empowerment content while market destroys PM efficacy signals.
behavioral_signals: All-hands says move fast; middle manager vetoes experiments.
emotional_political_dynamics: Cynicism masked as professionalism.
operational_adaptations: Publish decision log showing which IC bets shipped; celebrate reversals.
ai_era_implications: AI gives autonomy to everyone—efficacy gap widens without feedback discipline.

source_episode: Essential reading for product builders—part 1
source_quote_or_context: When execution is cheap, taste—not tooling—becomes the scarce differentiator between slop and products people love.
core_belief: More builders does not mean more good products.
operational_pattern: Invest in reference sets, design critique, and kill criteria before scaling AI output.
organizational_archetype: Small senior circle with veto on customer-facing quality.
hidden_assumptions: That volume of experiments discovers taste automatically.
pressure_scenarios: Competitor ships AI feature parity; board wants AI everywhere.
failure_modes: Feature zoo; brand dilution; users churn from inconsistent quality.
contradictions: Essential reading list elevates taste while ecosystem rewards shipping slop.
behavioral_signals: Metrics up on engagement hacks; NPS flat; design complaints dismissed.
emotional_political_dynamics: Design vs growth fight; PM caught optimizing clicks.
operational_adaptations: Taste rubric in PR review; fewer bigger bets with editorial bar.
ai_era_implications: Taste becomes hiring moat—tasteless orgs drown in AI-generated sameness.

source_episode: Essential reading for product builders—part 1
source_quote_or_context: Consuming AI content without building is pretend learning—it feels like progress while skill atrophy continues.
core_belief: Information diet is not capability building.
operational_pattern: Cap newsletter and podcast time with equal build hours; ship weekly micro-artifact.
organizational_archetype: PM who tracks build-to-consume ratio in public.
hidden_assumptions: That awareness equals adoption; that courses substitute for reps.
pressure_scenarios: Performance review asks for AI fluency; no time blocked to build.
failure_modes: Imposter syndrome hidden behind curated reading lists; no portfolio of work.
contradictions: Newsletter itself is consumption—meta tension with build-more advice.
behavioral_signals: Bookmarks hundreds of posts; repo empty; asks eng to prototype.
emotional_political_dynamics: FOMO anxiety; shame about not being technical enough.
operational_adaptations: Build buddy accountability; share failures not just highlights.
ai_era_implications: Learning splits: consumers vs builders—latter compound advantage.

source_episode: Essential reading for product builders—part 1
source_quote_or_context: The builders who win combine curiosity, agency, and communication—missing one collapses the triangle under AI load.
core_belief: Skills are necessary but attributes determine whether skills compound.
operational_pattern: Hire and coach for all three; do not promote brilliant curmudgeons or articulate passengers.
organizational_archetype: High-trust team with explicit feedback on communication gaps.
hidden_assumptions: That coding skill alone suffices; that communication is soft optional.
pressure_scenarios: Remote async; cross-functional AI projects; exec visibility scarce.
failure_modes: Brilliant builder nobody follows; great communicator no delivery.
contradictions: Timeless attributes advice in era obsessed with tool tips.
behavioral_signals: Silent high performers overlooked; loud mediocre promoted.
emotional_political_dynamics: Identity tied to being the smart one vs being effective.
operational_adaptations: 360 on communication; pairing curious juniors with agency mentors.
ai_era_implications: AI rewards communicators who can steer agents—curiosity without communication wastes tokens.

source_episode: Essential reading for product builders—part 1
source_quote_or_context: Build where market pull already exists—AI makes push cheap, so push without pull becomes noise squared.
core_belief: Distribution of attention is scarcer than distribution of code.
operational_pattern: Validate pull signals before building; use AI to amplify pull, not invent demand.
organizational_archetype: Founder-PM watching organic usage clusters before roadmap commit.
hidden_assumptions: That if we ship they will come; that AI demos create demand.
pressure_scenarios: Investor pressure for AI roadmap; internal pet projects.
failure_modes: Spray of AI features nobody asked for; burn on sales-led push.
contradictions: Builder empowerment narrative vs discipline of pull-first.
behavioral_signals: Usage flat while demo views spike; sales asks for custom AI.
emotional_political_dynamics: FOMO vs focus pain for leaders.
operational_adaptations: Hold AI ideas until three independent pull signals; kill push-only bets.
ai_era_implications: Pull detection becomes core PM analytics—agents generate, humans select for pull.

source_episode: Essential reading for product builders—part 1
source_quote_or_context: Consume both timeless principles and bleeding-edge tactics—middle-of-the-road product content ages instantly in AI waves.
core_belief: Career insurance is barbelled: classics plus frontier reps.
operational_pattern: Schedule deep reads on fundamentals plus weekly hands-on with newest tools; ignore mushy middle.
organizational_archetype: Operator with canon books plus daily agent experiments.
hidden_assumptions: That one newsletter subscription is enough; that fundamentals are boring.
pressure_scenarios: Tool churn weekly; strategy principles stable for decades.
failure_modes: Chasing tactics without judgment; preaching principles without shipping.
contradictions: Curated reading list is itself middle—reader must add extremes.
behavioral_signals: Only reads summaries; no primary sources; no reps.
emotional_political_dynamics: Intellectual snobbery vs hustle culture split.
operational_adaptations: Personal canon doc plus changelog of tools tried.
ai_era_implications: Barbell widens—timeless judgment even more valuable as tactics obsolesce weekly.

source_episode: Essential reading for product builders—part 1
source_quote_or_context: AI makes everyone a cook who can follow recipes—chefs who invent recipes and taste outcomes own the category.
core_belief: Recipe following scales; recipe invention is the moat.
operational_pattern: Force why prompts: why this architecture, why this metric, why now—before accepting AI recipe.
organizational_archetype: Team with chefs on platform and cooks on execution with clear ladder between.
hidden_assumptions: That prompting templates equal product management.
pressure_scenarios: Commodity features; race to same Copilot checklist.
failure_modes: Identical AI products; price war; no retention story.
contradictions: Teaches reading list while tools turn PMs into cooks by default.
behavioral_signals: Team ships competitor clone in a week; no differentiation.
emotional_political_dynamics: Senior chefs bored; junior cooks overconfident.
operational_adaptations: Chef time protected from ticket load; cooks mentored toward invention.
ai_era_implications: Organizations split chef strategists from cook operators—middle hollows faster.

source_episode: What people are vibe coding (and actually using)
source_quote_or_context: The dominant vibe-coded product is software for one person—the builder—flipping the venture scale assumption.
core_belief: Personal utility is a valid product category when build cost approaches zero.
operational_pattern: Ship for yourself first; expand only when others pull; resist premature SaaS packaging.
organizational_archetype: Solo builder with portfolio of micro-tools not one startup.
hidden_assumptions: That every tool must become a company; that n-of-1 cannot teach GTM.
pressure_scenarios: Investor asks for TAM; friends want your script.
failure_modes: Abandoned side projects; no maintenance; security holes in personal tools.
contradictions: Survey celebrates indie builders while ecosystem funds only scale plays.
behavioral_signals: Twitter demos; zero users besides author.
emotional_political_dynamics: Loneliness of builder; imposter vs founder identity.
operational_adaptations: Open-source personal tools; let community fork if pull appears.
ai_era_implications: Software long tail explodes—billions of n-of-1 apps, few winners take all.

source_episode: What people are vibe coding (and actually using)
source_quote_or_context: Vibe coders cluster on browser extensions because distribution is one click and scope is bounded—gravity well hides harder problems.
core_belief: Extension store is the path of least resistance for first ship.
operational_pattern: Use extensions to validate workflow pain; plan escape velocity to platform if traction.
organizational_archetype: Indie hackers shipping five extensions before one real business.
hidden_assumptions: That extension success transfers to mobile or enterprise automatically.
pressure_scenarios: Manifest V3 policy changes; store takedowns; API breakage.
failure_modes: Portfolio of 200-user extensions; no moat; support burden.
contradictions: Highlights extensions while AI enables full apps—gravity may be temporary.
behavioral_signals: Builders avoid backend because scary; everything is DOM hacks.
emotional_political_dynamics: Store review anxiety; fear of Google policy.
operational_adaptations: Treat extension as MVP wedge; document migration path early.
ai_era_implications: Extensions become agent attach points—new distribution for AI workflows.

source_episode: What people are vibe coding (and actually using)
source_quote_or_context: Non-engineers now describe software in plain English and remote-control an AI engineer—the role boundary is negotiation not job title.
core_belief: Intent expression separates builders from typists.
operational_pattern: Write acceptance criteria in English; let agent implement; review diff like a tech lead.
organizational_archetype: PM with production access via agent and design partner for taste.
hidden_assumptions: That no code means no responsibility for production incidents.
pressure_scenarios: Incident in prod from PM merge; security audit failure.
failure_modes: Blameless postmortem reveals PM shipped without review; eng revokes access.
contradictions: Celebrates PM builders while companies still deny repo access.
behavioral_signals: PMs secretly vibe code; eng discovers in outage.
emotional_political_dynamics: Territorial eng; PM empowerment shame.
operational_adaptations: Shared review norms; agent audit logs; blameless but accountable.
ai_era_implications: Plain-English engineering becomes literal job requirement for PMs.

source_episode: What people are vibe coding (and actually using)
source_quote_or_context: The strongest filter for vibe-coded products is daily self-use—if you do not use it, it is a demo not a product.
core_belief: Dogfooding is the only honest eval when build is free.
operational_pattern: Mandate seven-day self-use before sharing; kill if you reach for the old tool.
organizational_archetype: Builder who deletes more projects than they publish.
hidden_assumptions: That launch day usage predicts retention; that friends' praise counts.
pressure_scenarios: Social media pressure to announce; hackathon deadlines.
failure_modes: Ghost tools; broken auth you never hit; embarrassed silence after launch.
contradictions: Survey of what people use vs what they tweet—contradiction by design.
behavioral_signals: Product Hunt spike; author never opens app again.
emotional_political_dynamics: Ego attachment to announced projects.
operational_adaptations: Public build log with use metrics; shame OK for killed tools.
ai_era_implications: Self-use telemetry becomes default analytics for solo builders.

source_episode: What people are vibe coding (and actually using)
source_quote_or_context: Vibe coders report consolidating stacks because tool sprawl has higher cognitive cost than feature gaps.
core_belief: Fewer tools with deeper fluency beats infinite beta signups.
operational_pattern: Pick one IDE, one agent, one deploy path for a quarter; measure throughput not novelty.
organizational_archetype: Small team standardizing on Cursor plus one backend.
hidden_assumptions: That best-of-breed always wins; that FOMO misses nothing important.
pressure_scenarios: New model drop weekly; influencer stack posts daily.
failure_modes: Migration whiplash; lost context; no muscle memory.
contradictions: Newsletter ecosystem profits from tool churn while advising consolidation.
behavioral_signals: Calendar full of trying new apps; roadmap empty.
emotional_political_dynamics: Anxiety of being left behind.
operational_adaptations: Tool budget: one experiment slot per month; document why switched.
ai_era_implications: Consolidation winners capture agents—second-tool markets shrink.

source_episode: What people are vibe coding (and actually using)
source_quote_or_context: Many vibe-coded products find users accidentally—support forum post, reply guy, internal slack share—not deliberate GTM.
core_belief: Build in public works when artifact is genuinely useful, not when marketing is performative.
operational_pattern: Optimize for shareable moment of value; leave breadcrumbs in communities where pain is vocal.
organizational_archetype: Indie builder whose growth is 90% word of mouth from one use case.
hidden_assumptions: That growth can be planned like enterprise funnel; that SEO is dead.
pressure_scenarios: Platform algorithm change; community ban; copycat overnight.
failure_modes: One-hit wonder; cannot reproduce; burns out answering DMs.
contradictions: Accidental distribution praised while VCs demand repeatable GTM.
behavioral_signals: Viral tweet; no onboarding; churn 100%.
emotional_political_dynamics: Uncomfortable with sales identity.
operational_adaptations: Capture origin story in onboarding; double down on accidental use case.

source_episode: What people are vibe coding (and actually using)
source_quote_or_context: Survey highlights women building in public at rising rates—visibility does not equal funding or enterprise adoption yet.
core_belief: Democratized tools lower build barrier; institutions lag on capital and trust.
operational_pattern: Amplify builders' work with credit; design communities that reduce harassment tax of public building.
organizational_archetype: Diverse indie builders network sharing stacks and safety practices.
hidden_assumptions: That build access alone fixes representation in outcomes.

behavioral_signals: Women builders over-mentored, under-capitalized.
emotional_political_dynamics: Exhaustion from being symbol and builder at once.
operational_adaptations: Paid communities; sponsorship; intentional spotlight without tokenism.
ai_era_implications: Lower build barrier shifts fight to distribution and capital—new bottlenecks.

source_episode: An AI glossary
source_quote_or_context: Models are pretrained, then fine-tuned, then RLHF'd, then tool-connected—each layer adds capability and new failure modes.
core_belief: No single knob controls behavior; stack literacy prevents magical thinking.
operational_pattern: Map your product bet to which layer actually needs to change before retraining fantasies.
organizational_archetype: ML platform team educating PMs on what fine-tune vs prompt vs RAG solves.
hidden_assumptions: That bigger base model makes fine-tune unnecessary; that RLHF fixes all safety.
pressure_scenarios: Vendor promises one-click custom model; compliance asks for explainability.
failure_modes: Wrong layer investment; months lost fine-tuning when prompt plus eval would suffice.
contradictions: Glossary simplifies stack while vendors obscure for sales.
behavioral_signals: PMs say we need fine-tune without data; eng sighs.
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of looking dumb in ML meetings.
operational_adaptations: Decision tree poster in wiki; escalate to platform when layer unclear.
ai_era_implications: PMs must know ladder—budget and timeline conversations depend on it.

source_episode: An AI glossary
source_quote_or_context: Hallucination is often missing context presented as fluent completion—the fix is retrieval and scope, not shame.
core_belief: Confident wrong answers are a context engineering problem.
operational_pattern: Narrow task scope; ground with RAG; refuse to answer outside corpus; measure groundedness.
organizational_archetype: Support bot with strict corpus and escalation path.
hidden_assumptions: That users can prompt-engineer away bad retrieval; that bigger model ends hallucinations.
pressure_scenarios: Legal asks for guarantees; sales demos unconstrained.
failure_modes: Trust collapse after one high-profile wrong answer.
contradictions: Defines hallucination clinically while market uses it as moral panic word.
behavioral_signals: PM disables feature after one bug; eng wants eval investment.
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of shipping customer-facing AI.
operational_adaptations: Grounded eval suite; show citations in UI; human handoff triggers.
ai_era_implications: Hallucination management becomes standard PM skill like latency budgets.

source_episode: An AI glossary
source_quote_or_context: Agents sit on a spectrum from suggest to act to orchestrate—confusion on placement causes every AI disaster story.
core_belief: Agency level is a product decision with liability attached.
operational_pattern: Label every feature with spectrum position; upgrade only with eval evidence and rollback.
organizational_archetype: PM plus legal plus eng signing agency changelog.
hidden_assumptions: That users want full autonomy because demos look cool.
pressure_scenarios: Competitor launches autonomous refund bot.
failure_modes: Over-automation scandal; under-automation irrelevance.
contradictions: Glossary clarifies spectrum while marketing demands autonomous wording.
behavioral_signals: Slide says agent; product is autocomplete.
emotional_political_dynamics: Legal vs product vs eng triangle fights.
operational_adaptations: Public agency meter in release notes; customer setting to reduce autonomy.
ai_era_implications: Companies publish agent tiers like security SLAs—market matures.

source_episode: An AI glossary
source_quote_or_context: Teams over-build eval frameworks with dozens of judges—complexity becomes excuse to avoid human labeling.
core_belief: Simple high-signal evals beat ornate low-signal dashboards.
operational_pattern: Cap judges at handful; prefer human spot checks for new failure modes.
organizational_archetype: Lean eval team resisting enterprise QA theater.
hidden_assumptions: That more metrics equals more control.
pressure_scenarios: Consultant sells eval platform; integration takes quarter.
failure_modes: Dashboard green; users furious; maintenance hell.
contradictions: Sophisticated guide risks readers over-engineering.
behavioral_signals: Engineers build eval platform not evals.
emotional_political_dynamics: Pride in internal tooling over user outcomes.
operational_adaptations: Start with ten tests in pytest; grow only on proven pain.
ai_era_implications: Eval tooling consolidates—complexity losers abandon bespoke stacks.

source_episode: An AI glossary
source_quote_or_context: Production gives sparse thumbs and silence—most valuable eval signal is intentionally collected, not passively hoped for.
core_belief: Implicit feedback is insufficient for rare catastrophic failures.
operational_pattern: Instrument explicit feedback on high-risk paths; sample sessions; interview on failures.
organizational_archetype: Growth plus PM plus trust-safety sharing labeled incident set.
hidden_assumptions: That analytics dashboards replace evals; that silence means satisfaction.
pressure_scenarios: Rare wrong medical or legal advice; no thumbs down.
failure_modes: Catastrophic tail event; no prior test.
contradictions: Calls for human signal while products minimize friction to give feedback.
behavioral_signals: Low NPS response rate ignored.
emotional_political_dynamics: Legal blocks asking users about errors.
operational_adaptations: Targeted outreach after high-stakes sessions; bounty for failure reports.
ai_era_implications: Synthetic users plus real incidents hybridize sparse signal problem.

source_episode: An AI glossary
source_quote_or_context: Agent failures need step-level eval mapping—which tool call failed—not only final answer grading.
core_belief: End-state scores hide recoverable mid-chain bugs.
operational_pattern: Log trajectories; assert per-step constraints; replay with mocks.
organizational_archetype: Agent platform team providing trace UI for PM review.
hidden_assumptions: That final response quality is enough.
pressure_scenarios: Multi-step booking agent; payment succeeded but wrong date.

emotional_political_dynamics: Eng hoards trace tools.
operational_adaptations: PM trained on trace reading; step evals in shared repo.
ai_era_implications: Step-level eval standard for any agent touching money or PII.

source_episode: An AI glossary
source_quote_or_context: RLHF teaches models to please raters—which can diverge from truth, safety, or your product values.
core_belief: Helpful-sounding is not the same as correct or on-brand.
operational_pattern: Test for sycophancy and refusals; add product-specific evals beyond vendor defaults.
organizational_archetype: Trust and safety reviewing model upgrades for new failure modes.
hidden_assumptions: That base model alignment equals your alignment.
pressure_scenarios: Launch in regulated domain; model refuses valid tasks or lies politely.

contradictions: Explains RLHF limits while vendors imply safety solved.

emotional_political_dynamics: Ethics vs growth on personality tuning.
operational_adaptations: Custom rubric judges; A/B on tone with retention and truth metrics.
ai_era_implications: Every product needs RLHF-on-RLHF—layered alignment for brand.

source_episode: An AI glossary

operational_adaptations: Written policy on what vibe coding can touch; prod requires review.
ai_era_implications: Term splits—professional agentic engineering vs casual vibe coding.

source_episode: State of the product job market in 2025
source_quote_or_context: PM hiring recovers unevenly—senior strategy and AI-native builders rebound faster than mid-level coordinators.
core_belief: The barbell in PM employment is structural, not cyclical.
operational_pattern: Position as builder-strategist; document shipped artifacts; avoid resume-only coordination narrative.

hidden_assumptions: That 2023 layoffs were temporary blip; that old PM playbook returns.

failure_modes: Years of coordination experience discounted; age bias masked as skills gap.

operational_adaptations: Reskill publicly; target companies with AI product surface.
ai_era_implications: PM market splits permanently—coordination PM role shrinks.

source_episode: State of the product job market in 2025
source_quote_or_context: AI PM titles multiply faster than shared definition—hiring managers conflate prompt ownership, ML literacy, and classic PM.
core_belief: Title inflation hides whether role is strategy, evals, or demo theater.
operational_pattern: Ask which layer: data, evals, UX, GTM, or platform; match your proof points.

hidden_assumptions: That AI PM means one thing; that courses equal qualification.
pressure_scenarios: Recruiter keyword match; take-home ambiguous.

contradictions: Market creates AI PM roles faster than curricula.
behavioral_signals: Job description lists 40 skills; salary generic PM.
emotional_political_dynamics: Imposter syndrome for non-ML PMs.
operational_adaptations: Publish your AI PM thesis; narrow search to matching scope.
ai_era_implications: AI PM splinters into eval PM, agent PM, data PM—specialization accelerates.

source_episode: State of the product job market in 2025
source_quote_or_context: Remote is baseline for many PM roles again—but elite networks still concentrate advantage in hybrid hubs.
core_belief: Geography matters less for job access, more for career compound interest.
operational_pattern: Invest in remote-native visibility: writing, demos, async leadership proof.

hidden_assumptions: That remote equals equal opportunity; that async needs no skill.

emotional_political_dynamics: FOMO on hallway decisions.
operational_adaptations: Document decisions in public channels; fly to key summits quarterly.

source_episode: State of the product job market in 2025
source_quote_or_context: Despite remote hiring, network density in Bay Area still accelerates fundraising, hiring, and acquihire odds.

emotional_political_dynamics: Resentment of coastal centrism.
operational_adaptations: Paid communities bridging geography; intentional intro programs.
ai_era_implications: Virtual networks rise but gravity weakens slowly—plan accordingly.

source_episode: State of the product job market in 2025
source_quote_or_context: Layoffs slowed in 2025 but did not stop—survivor guilt and zero-sum internal competition persist.
core_belief: Stability headlines do not restore pre-2023 trust.

contradictions: Optimistic market report vs lived employee anxiety.
behavioral_signals: Managers stop 1:1 career talk; teams hoard work.
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of being next; competition not collaboration.

source_episode: State of the product job market in 2025
source_quote_or_context: Product engineer roles rise—hybrids who ship full stack with product judgment absorb work that was PM plus eng handoff.

source_episode: State of the product job market in 2025

source_episode: Beyond vibe checks: A PM's complete guide to evals

core_belief: Prompt optimization without measurement is superstition.

organizational_archetype: PM-owned eval suite reviewed with eng before any prompt change ships.
hidden_assumptions: That clever prompt fixes structural retrieval gap.

emotional_political_dynamics: PM vs eng blame ping-pong.
operational_adaptations: Eval-first change protocol in team wiki.
ai_era_implications: Prompt libraries commoditize—eval IP is moat.

source_episode: Beyond vibe checks: A PM's complete guide to evals
source_quote_or_context: Shipping AI without evals is like granting drivers licenses without road tests—fluency in chat is not competence in traffic.
core_belief: Society will demand proof of performance for consequential AI.
operational_pattern: Build driving test scenarios from production near-misses before scaling user count.
organizational_archetype: Regulated-adjacent product with published eval criteria.
hidden_assumptions: That beta label absolves quality; that users are the test.
pressure_scenarios: PR crisis; regulator inquiry; competitor publicizes your failures.
failure_modes: Injury metaphor becomes legal reality; brand collapse.
contradictions: Strong metaphor vs startups skipping tests for speed.
behavioral_signals: We are still learning used in incident postmortem.
emotional_political_dynamics: Legal wants sign-off; product wants ship.
operational_adaptations: Publish internal test pass rates; external trust follows.
ai_era_implications: Driving tests become certification industry for agents.

source_episode: Beyond vibe checks: A PM's complete guide to evals
source_quote_or_context: Eval inputs split across text, code, and multimodal—each modality needs different harness and failure taxonomy.
core_belief: One eval pipeline cannot judge what it cannot perceive.

organizational_archetype: Platform team providing modality-specific eval templates.
hidden_assumptions: That text judges suffice for vision features.
pressure_scenarios: Image feature shipped with only string asserts.
failure_modes: Silent failures on UI layout; accessibility regressions.
contradictions: Unified framework preached; reality requires specialization.
behavioral_signals: PM writes text-only evals for screenshot product.
emotional_political_dynamics: Modality experts siloed.
operational_adaptations: Hire or partner for modality depth; do not fake with GPT-only judges.
ai_era_implications: Multimodal eval tooling market explodes—PM specifies scenarios per sense.

source_episode: Beyond vibe checks: A PM's complete guide to evals
source_quote_or_context: A useful LLM judge needs task definition, rubric, exemplars, and aggregation—skipping a part produces theater metrics.
core_belief: Judges are products, not one-line prompts.
operational_pattern: Document all four parts; version judges like APIs; calibrate against humans.
organizational_archetype: Eval engineer pairing with PM on rubric workshops.
hidden_assumptions: That higher judge count improves signal.
pressure_scenarios: Board wants AI quality dashboard by quarter end.
failure_modes: Contradictory judges; false green; wasted tokens.
contradictions: Simplicity advice vs complex judge reality.
behavioral_signals: Copy-paste judge prompt from blog; scores nonsense.
emotional_political_dynamics: Dispute whether 4 or 5 is good enough.
operational_adaptations: Judge design doc required in PR template.
ai_era_implications: Judge marketplaces emerge—buy calibrated judges per domain.

source_episode: Beyond vibe checks: A PM's complete guide to evals
source_quote_or_context: LLM judges must agree with human experts roughly ninety percent on golden set before they scale—below that you automate noise.
core_belief: Un calibrated automation accelerates wrong conclusions.
operational_pattern: Label hundred cases with two humans; tune judge until kappa acceptable; only then auto-score.
organizational_archetype: Human labeling squad for high-stakes eval paths.
hidden_assumptions: That any agreement is fine; that judges replace humans entirely.
pressure_scenarios: Cost pressure to remove labelers; CEO wants fully automated QA.
failure_modes: Automated greenwashing; ship regressions at scale.
contradictions: Calibration rigor vs move fast narrative.
behavioral_signals: Celebrate high automated score; humans disagree in spot check.
emotional_political_dynamics: Labelers undervalued; PMs skip hard labeling.
operational_adaptations: Budget human calibration; treat as COGS not R&D optional.
ai_era_implications: Calibration-as-a-service for judges—standard enterprise buy.

source_episode: Beyond vibe checks: A PM's complete guide to evals
source_quote_or_context: Eval work is a loop: ship, fail, capture, add to suite, fix—not a one-time checklist before launch.
core_belief: Quality compounds through repository of failures.
operational_pattern: Every incident becomes eval within 48 hours; weekly eval review in product meeting.
organizational_archetype: Team with eval backlog prioritized like bug backlog.
hidden_assumptions: That launch evals are sufficient; that failures are shameful not data.
pressure_scenarios: Model vendor update; silent behavior drift.

contradictions: Iteration preached; roadmaps skip eval maintenance sprints.
behavioral_signals: Postmortem without new test case.
emotional_political_dynamics: Blame users for edge cases.
operational_adaptations: Eval debt labeled on dashboard; OKR on regression rate.
ai_era_implications: Continuous eval ops role appears—SRE for probabilistic products.

operational_adaptations: Make eval wins visible in all-hands; tie bonuses to regression metrics.

source_episode: A guide to AI prototyping for product managers
source_quote_or_context: PM prototyping tools split into chat ideation, browser builders, and IDE agents—using wrong tier wastes time or creates false confidence.

source_quote_or_context: Browser-native builders like Bolt hit walls on auth, backend, and state—fine for narrative demos, dangerous for technical validation.

source_episode: A guide to AI prototyping for product managers
source_quote_or_context: Best PM prototypes start with written reflection on user, risk, and decision—code without thought is expensive doodling.

hidden_assumptions: That building is thinking; that speed requires skipping reflection.

source_episode: A guide to AI prototyping for product managers
source_quote_or_context: Context-switching between Slack and prototype destroys flow—batch prototype blocks like deep work.

hidden_assumptions: That five-minute prompts between meetings count as building.

emotional_political_dynamics: Manager schedules over prototype block.
operational_adaptations: Team norm: prototype blocks sacred; async responses delayed.
ai_era_implications: Agents shorten build time—batching still matters for judgment.

source_episode: A guide to AI prototyping for product managers

operational_pattern: Include persona, data examples, error states, and non-goals in every build prompt.
organizational_archetype: PM with prompt library tagged by product area.
hidden_assumptions: That model will infer your product sense.
pressure_scenarios: Demo looks like every other AI SaaS template.
failure_modes: Stakeholders cannot tell your product apart; user test useless.
contradictions: More tools lower skill bar while raising taste bar.
behavioral_signals: Copy generic prompt from blog.
emotional_political_dynamics: Frustration when output bland.
operational_adaptations: Critique prompts like PRDs; iterate text before regenerating code.
ai_era_implications: Prompt specificity becomes versioned spec—feeds evals later.

source_episode: A guide to AI prototyping for product managers
source_quote_or_context: Prototypes need git-like checkpoints—without rollback, one bad prompt destroys afternoon and morale.
core_belief: Exploration requires reversible experiments.
operational_pattern: Save version before each major prompt; name checkpoints after hypothesis.
organizational_archetype: PM using Lovable or Cursor history religiously.
hidden_assumptions: That undo is enough; that version control is eng-only.
pressure_scenarios: Broken state; cannot reproduce good version; demo tomorrow.
failure_modes: Scramble rewrite; miss sleep; worse judgment.
contradictions: Easy tools hide need for discipline they still require.
behavioral_signals: Never saves; blames tool.
emotional_political_dynamics: Anxiety about lost work.
operational_adaptations: Teach checkpoint habit in onboarding; shared team naming convention.
ai_era_implications: Agent sessions auto-checkpoint—PM skill becomes hypothesis labeling.

source_episode: A guide to AI prototyping for product managers
source_quote_or_context: PRDs that specify only frontend lie about scope—AI makes frontend free while backend becomes the real schedule.
core_belief: Visible UI is not the product boundary.
operational_pattern: Prototype with fake data; document backend assumptions; eng reviews integration risks early.
organizational_archetype: PM partnering eng on integration spike before commitment.
hidden_assumptions: That clickable PRD replaces technical discovery.
pressure_scenarios: CEO commits date from demo.
failure_modes: Backend surprise doubles timeline; blame on eng bad faith.
contradictions: Frontend-only PRD warning in prototyping era when frontend is cheapest.
behavioral_signals: PRD screenshots only; no API mention.
emotional_political_dynamics: Eng disengaged until too late.
operational_adaptations: Integration appendix required on every PRD; eng signoff.
ai_era_implications: PRDs shrink—replaced by prototype plus contract tests.

source_episode: A guide to AI prototyping for product managers
source_quote_or_context: Typical path: start in Lovable for speed, graduate to Cursor when integration and files matter—graduation is skill not failure.
core_belief: Tool graduation is expected lifecycle not indictment of first tool.
operational_pattern: Set graduation triggers: need real API, monorepo, or test suite.
organizational_archetype: PM team celebrating Lovable to Cursor promotions in show-and-tell.
hidden_assumptions: That staying in browser builder is laziness.
pressure_scenarios: Premature Cursor use burns time on env issues.
failure_modes: Stuck in Lovable past viability; or Cursor paralysis day one.
contradictions: Clear path vs identity attachment to first tool.
behavioral_signals: Refuses to leave tool that raised them.
emotional_political_dynamics: Eng sneers at Lovable alumni.
operational_adaptations: Mentor pairing for graduation week; checklist for export.
ai_era_implications: Tools chain into pipelines—export between vendors standardizes.

source_episode: Product manager is an unfair role. So work unfairly.
source_quote_or_context: The PM job is won in live meetings—not async docs—because alignment and politics move at the speed of human reaction.
core_belief: Documentation without presence loses to competitors who show up prepared and adaptive.
operational_pattern: Pre-brief outcomes, attend live, follow up in writing within an hour while memory is hot.
organizational_archetype: PM who treats calendar as primary work surface not Slack.
hidden_assumptions: That great Notion wins wars; that async-first companies removed this truth.
pressure_scenarios: Hybrid schedules; dominant voices; exec skip-levels.
failure_modes: Outmaneuvered in room; doc never read; surprise decisions.
contradictions: AI write not read while insisting meetings still matter.
behavioral_signals: PM sends doc instead of attending; wonders why decision changed.
emotional_political_dynamics: Introverts told to perform extroversion; burnout.
operational_adaptations: Meeting prep agent for scenarios; human still in room.
ai_era_implications: Agents prep; humans negotiate—meeting skill premium rises.

source_episode: Product manager is an unfair role. So work unfairly.
source_quote_or_context: Short Loom videos under sixty seconds replace long meetings for async clarity—brevity forces one decision per video.
core_belief: Respect for attention is a competitive advantage.
operational_pattern: Record one decision, one ask; link in Slack; no preamble apology tours.
organizational_archetype: PM famous for crisp Looms leadership actually watches.
hidden_assumptions: That longer equals more professional; that everyone watches.
pressure_scenarios: Global team; timezone pain.
failure_modes: Ten-minute rambles never played; rework requests multiply.
contradictions: Unfair work advice includes human video in AI era.
behavioral_signals: PM writes essay thread instead of Loom.
emotional_political_dynamics: Camera shyness; perfectionism retakes.
operational_adaptations: Template: context ten seconds, demo thirty, ask twenty.
ai_era_implications: AI avatars generate Looms—authentic short human clips still trust winners.

source_episode: Product manager is an unfair role. So work unfairly.
source_quote_or_context: Protect first morning hours from Slack—or reactive PM work consumes the day before strategic work begins.
core_belief: Availability defaults are enemy of depth.
operational_pattern: Delay Slack until post-deep-work block; set cultural expectation with manager.
organizational_archetype: Team with documented quiet mornings.
hidden_assumptions: That responsiveness equals dedication; that DMs are emergencies.
pressure_scenarios: Cross-timezone pings at 6am; exec DMs culture.
failure_modes: Always behind; no roadmap thinking; resentment.
contradictions: DMs are devil while newsletter lives on Slack ecosystem.
behavioral_signals: Green dot obsession; anxiety when offline.
emotional_political_dynamics: Manager punishes slow response.
operational_adaptations: Team charter for response SLAs; execs use email for true urgent.
ai_era_implications: Agents triage Slack—human block still needed for hard calls.

source_episode: Product manager is an unfair role. So work unfairly.
source_quote_or_context: Unfair advantage comes from building a mini-team of allies—chief of staff energy, design partner, eng whisperer—not solo heroics.
core_belief: PM output is network output.
operational_pattern: Invest relationships before you need favors; reciprocate with clarity and credit.
organizational_archetype: PM with informal coalition across functions.
hidden_assumptions: That merit alone drives decisions; that politics is optional.
pressure_scenarios: Reorg; hostile stakeholder; priority conflict.
failure_modes: Isolated PM loses; coalition PM wins resource.
contradictions: Unfair play framed while preaching integrity—tension is real.
behavioral_signals: Surprised when no eng sponsor.
emotional_political_dynamics: Disdain for politics labeled beneath me.
operational_adaptations: Map stakeholders quarterly; deliberate reciprocity.
ai_era_implications: Agent prep for stakeholders—human relationship still decisive.

source_episode: Product manager is an unfair role. So work unfairly.
source_quote_or_context: Decisions made in DMs create shadow alignment—public channels preserve fairness and audit trail.
core_belief: Private speed today becomes public conflict tomorrow.
operational_pattern: Push decisions to threads; summarize outcomes in writing in channel.
organizational_archetype: Healthy org with DM for social not strategy.
hidden_assumptions: That DM is faster therefore better; that transparency slows you down.
pressure_scenarios: Exec prefers DM; culture of backchannels.
failure_modes: Surprise launch objections; team learns decision late.
contradictions: Slack morning block vs DMs devil—same tool, different discipline.
behavioral_signals: PM brags about exec DM access.
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear of public disagreement.
operational_adaptations: Ask can we move this to channel; leader modeling.
ai_era_implications: Agents summarize public channels—DM culture harder to audit.

source_episode: Product manager is an unfair role. So work unfairly.
source_quote_or_context: Collect proof of impact like scrapbook—metrics, quotes, artifacts—because unfair roles require self-advocacy no system provides.
core_belief: If you do not record wins, they did not happen politically.
operational_pattern: Weekly scrapbook entry: outcome, evidence, lesson; use in reviews and interviews.
organizational_archetype: PM with running brag doc shared selectively.
hidden_assumptions: That good work speaks for itself; that humility requires hiding impact.
pressure_scenarios: Layoff; manager change; promo committee.
failure_modes: Forgotten contributions; weaker politician promoted.
contradictions: Unfair advice acknowledges system unfairness.
behavioral_signals: No metrics; vague bullets.
emotional_political_dynamics: Discomfort boasting.
operational_adaptations: Template shared in team; normalize evidence-based advocacy.
ai_era_implications: Agents draft scrapbook from commits—human curates narrative.

source_episode: Product manager is an unfair role. So work unfairly.
source_quote_or_context: Use AI to produce artifacts others consume; do not use AI to consume instead of reading—thinking must stay human on inputs.
core_belief: Automation of output without input comprehension doubles risk.
operational_pattern: AI drafts memo; you read source data; you attend meeting; never AI-summary-only for decisions.
organizational_archetype: PM with strict read-primary-sources rule.
hidden_assumptions: That summarization equals understanding.
pressure_scenarios: Huge doc drop; AI summary wrong on nuance.
failure_modes: Bad decision from skipped reading; accountability still yours.
contradictions: AI productivity guide warns against AI over-read.
behavioral_signals: Paste summary into leadership deck; caught in Q&A.
emotional_political_dynamics: Time pressure guilt.
operational_adaptations: Block reading time before writing time in calendar.
ai_era_implications: Market floods AI-written slop—human-read signal becomes trust marker.

source_episode: Product manager is an unfair role. So work unfairly.
source_quote_or_context: Unfair performance requires rest—chronic exhaustion makes you the slowest negotiator in the room.
core_belief: Sustainable intensity beats performative hustle.
operational_pattern: Protect sleep and recovery like meeting blocks; sprint then recover.
organizational_archetype: Senior PM modeling rest without apology.
hidden_assumptions: That burnout is badge of honor; that rest is for after promotion.
pressure_scenarios: Crunch; family guilt; layoff fear.
failure_modes: Snap in meeting; poor judgment; health cost.
contradictions: Hustle culture critique inside hustle newsletter economy.
behavioral_signals: Brags all-nighters; quality drops.
emotional_political_dynamics: Shame about boundaries.
operational_adaptations: Team recovery days; no-meeting Friday real not fake.
ai_era_implications: Agents reduce grunt work—rest becomes competitive edge not luxury.

source_episode: Product manager is an unfair role. So work unfairly.
source_quote_or_context: The PM role is structurally unfair—accepting that lets you stop waiting for permission and play offense within ethics.
core_belief: Fairness is not incoming; leverage is built.
operational_pattern: Stop expecting equitable process; invest in skills, coalition, and artifacts that change outcomes.
organizational_archetype: Veteran PM mentoring juniors on system reality without cynicism.
hidden_assumptions: That HR process is neutral; that patience alone rewards.
pressure_scenarios: Bias; reorg politics; favorite exec projects.
failure_modes: Bitterness paralysis; unethical shortcuts.
contradictions: Title says work unfairly—not cheat.
behavioral_signals: Cynical gaming; ethical operators feel naive.
emotional_political_dynamics: Moral injury.
operational_adaptations: Clear ethical bright lines; unfair within integrity.
ai_era_implications: AI widens gap—ethical unfair players compound faster than waiters.

source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
source_quote_or_context: Duolingo reignited growth by fixing retention before buying traffic—acquisition on a leaky bucket is vanity.
core_belief: Retention is the honestest growth metric.
operational_pattern: Pause paid scale until core loop retention improves; reallocate to product quality and habit.
organizational_archetype: Growth team subordinate to learning outcomes metric.
hidden_assumptions: That CAC solves everything; that viral marketing fixes bad product.
pressure_scenarios: Board pressure for user count; competitor ad spend.
failure_modes: Burn on ads; churn hides; unit economics collapse.
contradictions: Classic growth vs Mazal retention-first turnaround story.
behavioral_signals: Marketing celebrates installs; retention flat.
emotional_political_dynamics: Growth vs product blame game.
operational_adaptations: Hold acquisition spend gate on CURR or retention threshold.
ai_era_implications: AI personalization raises retention bar—acquisition without it wastes more.

source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
source_quote_or_context: CURR—current user retention rate—became north star aligning product, marketing, and monetization on kept users not touched users.
core_belief: One metric reduces religious wars if chosen correctly.
operational_pattern: Define CURR precisely; report weekly; tie experiments to CURR delta not vanity.
organizational_archetype: Duolingo-style org with single hierarchical metric.
hidden_assumptions: That DAU alone drives stock story; that metrics are apolitical.
pressure_scenarios: Exec wants MAU for narrative; team optimizes wrong loop.
failure_modes: Submetric gaming; short-term CURR hurt by monetization push.
contradictions: North star purity vs multiple competing goals at scale.
behavioral_signals: Teams claim CURR win on definitional change.
emotional_political_dynamics: Metric politics in planning.
operational_adaptations: Lock definition; audit changes; pair with learning outcome metric.
ai_era_implications: AI products need CURR analog—session return not token count.

source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
source_quote_or_context: Personalized difficulty and content path drove retention—one-size-fits-all lesson is operational malpractice.
core_belief: Learning products must adapt faster than user boredom.
operational_pattern: Invest in adaptation engine; A/B path algorithms; measure learning not just time-on-app.
organizational_archetype: Data science embedded in curriculum squad.
hidden_assumptions: That content library size equals quality; that teachers scale linearly.
pressure_scenarios: Model costs; cold start for new languages.
failure_modes: Generic path; bored advanced users; overwhelmed beginners quit.
contradictions: Human teachers vs AI adaptation—both needed at scale.
behavioral_signals: Static course envy from competitors.
emotional_political_dynamics: Pedagogy vs ML team conflicts.
operational_adaptations: Expose adaptation visibly so users trust system.
ai_era_implications: LLM tutoring makes adaptation table stakes—static courses die.

source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
source_quote_or_context: Social competition works among strangers with similar levels—not friends only—because embarrassment dynamics differ.
core_belief: Social design must match relationship graph reality.
operational_pattern: League strangers not friend leaderboard; reset cycles to reduce demotivation.
organizational_archetype: Game designers tuning league segmentation weekly.
hidden_assumptions: That Facebook friend graph is required for virality.
pressure_scenarios: Privacy backlash on friend compare.
failure_modes: Demotivated users quit; toxic competition.
contradictions: Stranger leagues success vs schools banning competitive ranking.
behavioral_signals: Users cheat leagues; bots in leaderboard.
emotional_political_dynamics: Parents vs growth on child competition.
operational_adaptations: Segment leagues; anti-cheat; opt-out paths.
ai_era_implications: AI opponents in leagues—new fairness debates emerge.

source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
source_quote_or_context: Notifications are a scarce trust channel—over-notification trains ignore and kills retention lever.
core_belief: Every push is a withdrawal from a finite trust account.
operational_pattern: Cap frequency; personalize timing; measure uninstall and mute not just open rate.
organizational_archetype: Growth team with notification budget per user per week.
hidden_assumptions: That more reminders equals more learning.
pressure_scenarios: Quarter push revenue target.
failure_modes: Notification blindness; brand annoyance; OS-level mute.
contradictions: Streak mechanics depend on notifications—tension with protection.
behavioral_signals: Open rate up; retention down.
emotional_political_dynamics: User backlash on TikTok.
operational_adaptations: Model optimal send time; kill campaigns that spike unsubscribes.
ai_era_implications: AI personalized nudges can restore trust or destroy faster.

source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
source_quote_or_context: Streaks compound habit—but also create anxiety and brittle motivation—operational balance required.
core_belief: Loss aversion drives return visits until it drives shame quit.
operational_pattern: Streak freeze; repair mechanics; pair streak with real learning minutes.
organizational_archetype: Live ops team monitoring streak-induced churn cohorts.
hidden_assumptions: That infinite streak growth is always good.
pressure_scenarios: Competitor copies streak without repair.
failure_modes: Burnout users leave permanently; negative press on dark patterns.
contradictions: Streak hero story vs mental health criticism.
behavioral_signals: Users cheat timezone; support tickets explode.
emotional_political_dynamics: Ethics vs growth OKR fight.
operational_adaptations: Streak ethics review; transparent mechanics.
ai_era_implications: AI coaches may replace dumb streaks with competence streaks.

source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
source_quote_or_context: Duolingo balance: big swings on new bets while optimizing core—both required; either alone stalls.
core_belief: Exploration and exploitation are concurrent jobs not phases.
operational_pattern: Portfolio budget: percent resources on swings; percent on CURR optimization; review quarterly.
organizational_archetype: Leadership team with explicit swing list and owner.
hidden_assumptions: That optimization is boring so skip it; that swings excuse core neglect.
pressure_scenarios: Investor wants new product line; core decaying.
failure_modes: Swing fails; core weakened; no floor.
contradictions: Turnaround story includes both discipline types.
behavioral_signals: All swings no ops; or all ops no innovation.
emotional_political_dynamics: Credit fights between teams.
operational_adaptations: Swing postmortems; core regression tests on CURR.
ai_era_implications: AI enables more swings—without optimization discipline companies die faster.

source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
source_quote_or_context: Gardenscapes moves counter took months, changed nothing on retention, and the team disbanded—neutral results are worse than failure because they consume morale.
core_belief: Borrowed mechanics without strategic fit waste quarters and teach the wrong lesson if you do not postmortem context.
operational_pattern: Kill neutral experiments fast; run three-question borrow framework before build; document why similarity masked difference.
organizational_archetype: Gamification team copying mobile games after exec buy-in.
hidden_assumptions: That visual similarity to hit games implies mechanic transfer.
pressure_scenarios: Pressure to show gamification progress after DAU stall.
failure_modes: Team dissolution drama; abandoned idea without learning captured.
contradictions: Retention-first thesis coexists with months spent on wrong mechanic.
behavioral_signals: Expectant launch monitoring; silence on metrics.
emotional_political_dynamics: Deflation and blame after neutral read.
operational_adaptations: Mandatory adaptation memo before any borrowed mechanic ships.
ai_era_implications: AI makes copying mechanics faster—neutral tests multiply without discipline.

source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
source_quote_or_context: Uber-style referral gave free Super month but best active users already had premium—strategy needed advocates it structurally excluded.
core_belief: Referral economics must reward the people who will actually refer, not hypothetical users.
operational_pattern: Map advocate identity before choosing reward; subscription products need different playbook than pay-as-you-go rides.
organizational_archetype: Acquisition team after gamification burn.
hidden_assumptions: That viral playbooks transfer across business models.
pressure_scenarios: 3% new-user lift insufficient for breakthrough.
failure_modes: Plateau; wasted iterations; best users ignored.
contradictions: Uber inspiration opening vs Duolingo failure—same article.
behavioral_signals: Premium users ineligible for reward.
emotional_political_dynamics: Frustration doubling down on referrals.
operational_adaptations: Reward status or currency power users lack—not redundant subscription month.
ai_era_implications: AI could personalize referral rewards—still requires advocate fit analysis.

source_episode: How Duolingo reignited user growth
source_quote_or_context: CURR impact on DAU was six times its impact on MAU—optimizing the wrong headline metric misallocates years of team effort.
core_belief: North star choice changes which projects win; DAU and MAU levers are not interchangeable.
operational_pattern: Report lever rankings per metric; align team to one primary growth metric per phase; defer MAU vectors until DAU model saturated.
organizational_archetype: Executive debate on investor narrative vs operational focus.
hidden_assumptions: That improving DAU automatically satisfies MAU story.
pressure_scenarios: IPO pressure for MAU; board asks for both.
failure_modes: Wrong team rewarded; MAU stalls while DAU soars.
contradictions: Focused DAU for years knowing MAU needs later bets—explicit tradeoff.
behavioral_signals: Sensitivity tables in growth model reviews.
emotional_political_dynamics: MAU paranoia intentionally deferred.
operational_adaptations: Publish which metric each experiment targets before ship.
ai_era_implications: AI analytics make multi-metric modeling easier—choosing one primary metric still required.
